_description:
Reviewer-curated manifest-level overrides. Three independent mechanisms:
  1. 'flips' — tokens whose CT + masks need spatial AP-flipping for visual consistency (e.g., normalizing all cases to a prone display orientation). Reflected across CT's world-Y bbox center via 3D resampling.
  2. 'label_swaps' — tokens whose mask file has anatomically inverted label VALUES from upstream source (e.g., CTPelvic1K had hip labels 2/3 placed on the wrong anatomical sides for these specific files). Voxels stay where they are; only label values get remapped (8↔9 for hips after export-time class remap).
  3. 'exclusions' — tokens to drop entirely from the output manifest because the mask/series pairing is fundamentally wrong.
A single token can appear in BOTH flips AND label_swaps; the spatial flip applies first, then the label remap. Tokens not listed in any section pass through unchanged.
_reviewer_instructions: 1. Run Stage 2 once with empty flips / label_swaps / exclusions lists.
2. Inspect data/qc_figures/ to identify cases needing flips vs exclusions.
3. Add flip entries for AP-inverted-but-correctly-paired cases.
4. Add exclusion entries for cases with mismatched mask/CT pairings.
5. Re-run Stage 2; Step C picks up flips and exclusions.
7. Run Stage 3 (export) and Stage 4 (TS benchmark + L/R audit).
8. For tokens flagged FLIPPED by the L/R audit (no-swap Dice ≈ 0,
swap Dice ≈ 0.93), confirm via axial QC images that the labels
are anatomically inverted in the CT. If yes, add to label_swaps
with appropriate swap pairs (typically [[8, 9]] for hip labels).
9. Re-run Stage 2; Step C applies the label remap on the same
10. Re-run Stage 3 + audit to confirm 0 FLIPPED.
_sides_field: Optional 'sides' list on FLIP entries controls which files get flipped:
omitted (default)  : all applicable files for the case's match_type
fused       -> CT + spine mask + pelvic mask
['spine']          : flip ONLY the spine CT + spine mask
['pelvic']         : flip ONLY the pelvic CT + pelvic mask
['spine','pelvic'] : flip both sides on a separate case (rare)
For fused / spine_only / pelvic_only, 'sides' is ignored.
_label_swap_field: Each label_swap entry has these fields:
token    : patient_token to apply to (must match manifest)
swap     : list of [a, b] pairs to swap. Each pair is bidirectional
(a -> b AND b -> a). Multiple pairs apply in parallel
Use UNIFIED label values (post-export class remap):
Determines which placed mask file gets the swap.
For hip labels 8/9 the target should be 'pelvic'.
Note: swap operates on placed mask values (CTPelvic1K
swap entry should reference unified post-export values
so the swap composes cleanly with the export remap.
whatever values are in the placed mask file; for hip

exclusions.notes: Spine mask is totally misaligned with bone on the CT. This is not AP inversion — flipping will not fix it. Likely cause: place_fused_masks.py picked a wrong-series candidate as the best spine match for this patient, OR the CTSpine1K annotation references a scan that was never reliably paired. Excluding from dataset pending manual re-investigation.

flips.notes: Fused; visually inverted (post-PIR appearance was supine, normalizing to prone-style display). CT + spine + pelvic flip together.
Separate. Spine side inverted; pelvic side correctly oriented. Flip spine CT + spine mask only.
Fused; visually inverted. CT + spine + pelvic flip together.
Fused supine; pelvic mass anterior of body middle by ~40mm. Visually confirmed inverted across sagittal + coronal + axial views. body-center detector delta_posterior_mm = -40.9.
Separate. Spine side inverted; pelvic side correct. Flip spine CT + spine mask only.
Fused; visually inverted. CT + spine + pelvic flip together.
Separate. Spine side inverted; pelvic side correct. Flip spine CT + spine mask only.
Pelvic-only; inverted. Flip pelvic CT + pelvic mask.
Separate. Pelvic side inverted; spine side correct. Flip pelvic CT + pelvic mask only.

label_swaps.notes: Source CTPelvic1K mask had hip labels 2 and 3 placed on anatomically inverted sides (label 2 was on patient-RIGHT hip, label 3 on patient-LEFT). Verified via TS-vs-GT hip Dice audit (no-swap=0.000, swap=0.94) and confirmed via axial visual QC. The CT data is correct; only mask label values are remapped. Composes with the AP flip listed above for this token — the flip applies first to the placed pelvic mask, then this swap relabels values 2 <-> 3 on the flipped output.
Source CTPelvic1K mask had hip labels 2 and 3 placed on anatomically inverted sides. Verified via TS-vs-GT hip Dice audit (no-swap=0.000, swap=0.92) and axial visual QC.

Source CTPelvic1K mask had hip labels 2 and 3 placed on anatomically inverted sides. Verified via TS-vs-GT hip Dice audit (no-swap=0.000, swap=0.93) and axial visual QC. Independently verified by checking world-X centroids of TS hip predictions (TS hip_left at world-X=-56.9) against placed mask label centroids.
Source CTPelvic1K mask had hip labels 2 and 3 placed on anatomically inverted sides. Verified via TS-vs-GT hip Dice audit (no-swap=0.000, swap=0.91) and axial visual QC.
